conclusion: Per-row scratch-only replay is not the c8 grouped/fullnative fix. It worsens full-attention stage failures, so the c8 rowchunk2/fullnative split remains tied to row grouping/composition rather than a direct scratch-only boundary.

interpretation.contrast_to_c2: Prior c2 scratch-only was a green controlled split, but c8 grouped/no-rowchunk needs rowchunk semantics or a different grouped full-attention composition fix; simply replaying scratch setup per row is not safe.
interpretation.negative_result: Scratch-only per-row replay does not transfer the prior c2 hidden fix to c8 fullnative. It is mismatch_found with hidden+token failures, keeps layer4 attn_input drift, and adds a large layer3 full-attention gate divergence (max_abs 7.73046875, 4096 elements over atol).
interpretation.next_target: Continue toward direct grouped full-attention output/composition or rowchunk direct-delta analysis rather than scratch-only replay.

prompt_verifier.evidence: PASS: focused c8 scratch/batch-setup diagnostic directly tests a previously green c2 hidden split and eliminates it for c8 with concrete artifact evidence; no performance claim.
